title: Cheilanthes auriculata
taxonomy: Cheilanthes auriculata is classified as a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Cheilanthes, which comprises several species of rock ferns. The genus Cheilanthes is further divided into several sections, with C. auriculata placed in the section Cheilanthes. This classification helps in distinguishing C. auriculata from other related species. The classification also provides valuable information about its evolutionary history and genetic relationships with other fern species.

intro: Cheilanthes auriculata, also known as Golden Rasp fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Pteridaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its potential to reduce inflammation and treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Cheilanthes auriculata include flavonoids, phenolic compounds, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
